subject: Computer Networking
title: BGP and Autonomous Systems
description: A short lesson to help you apply BGP and Autonomous Systems.

hook.text: By the late 1980s, the internet was many networks. They needed a way to share route information at their borders.

buildup.text: BGP — Border Gateway Protocol — was introduced in 1989. Each network, called an AS, announces its reachable addresses.

discovery.text: BGP lets each AS choose paths based on policy, not just shortest distance. Business relationships influence routing.

twist.text: BGP was built on trust. A misconfigured AS can accidentally hijack traffic for millions of users.

climax.text: BGP still runs the internet's core routing today. It's been patched and extended, never replaced.

punchline.text: BGP holds the internet together with trust and announcements.

quiz.question: What does BGP manage between autonomous systems?
options: Assignment of IP addresses to devices
